name: Dionne Warwick
abstract: Marie Dionne Warwick ( dee-ON WOR-wik; née Warrick; born December 12, 1940) is an American singer, actress, and television host. During her decades long career, Warwick has won many awards, including six Grammy Awards.

about_quotes.speaker: Vi Redd
about_quotes.evidence: I liked some of Burt Bacharach’s things that he did with Dionne Warwick.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vi Redd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Dionne Warwick. I did that for almost two years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Dionne Warwick and I were – we were great fans of pinochle. We would travel miles to be able to play together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: And he eventually ended up with Dionne Warwick three years ago, or 4 years ago, and he was doing some writing for her... He was the leader with a small group that she would take out

about_quotes.speaker: Horn Shirley
about_quotes.evidence: Dionne Warwick comes every night I‘m there, because all the singers and the writers and all come out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Horn Shirley — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: Dionne Warwick, James Brown, Marvin Gaye. I actually played with Michael Jackson and Janet Jackson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024
